title: Evotrochis odorata
taxonomy: Evotrochis odorata is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Australia. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. The genus Evotrochis is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae, which comprises about 15 genera of orchids. Evotrochis odorata is further classified as a member of the tribe Diurideae. Its classification is also sometimes referred to in the context of the larger group called Asparagales, which includes various monocotyledonous flowering plants.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
